found_files.evidence_text: The local HTML mirror contains narrative Replications & Reversals entries. A conservative scan produced 10 parseable D/N-like rows and 8 rows with replication N greater than original N, but the page is not a stable row-level export and several entries mix multiple original studies, critiques, meta-analyses, or alternative row grains.
found_files.why_relevant: FORRT Reversals is a plausible source of additional original/replication D/N assertions, but the available local artifact is narrative HTML rather than a clean source-result table.

found_files.evidence_text: The mirrored FReD workbook has source/result columns including original and replication references, DOI fields, study identifiers, original N, original effect value, and original effect type. Focused searches found overlap for several FORRT Reversals labels, including conjunction bias and door-in-the-face.
found_files.why_relevant: FReD is the main dedupe surface for FORRT-style psychology replication rows.

fields_or_columns_seen: Short description of the main phenomenon/hypothesis of the replication study
Original effect size converted to Bravais Pearson correlation
Replication effect size converted to Bravais Pearson correlation
Link to the OSF project or post-completion results report
found_files.evidence_text: The OSC/FReD template contains rownumber 104 for the fluency/psychological-distance finding, with original sample size 236 and replication sample size 1146. This matches the same replication route as the FORRT Reversals fluency entry, although FORRT's narrative selected original N 70 from Experiment 3.
found_files.why_relevant: This workbook confirms at least one FORRT candidate is already present in an existing machine-readable source family at a different original-side grain.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: data/raw/replication_projects/lead_harvest/fred_diff_2024/osf_11__220621_red_template_osc2015.xlsx sheet Daten rownumber 104; root table also has the OSC 2015 fluency row.
candidate_dedupe.original_effect: FORRT narrative: d = 0.26; OSC/FReD template: r = 0.127392361
candidate_dedupe.replication_effect: FORRT narrative: d = 0.04; OSC/FReD template: r = 0.018376516
candidate_dedupe.notes: Same phenomenon and replication route, but different original-side row grain. Treat as duplicate or alternate source support, not an automatic new row.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: data/raw/replication_projects/fred/FReD.xlsx fred_id CORE_113 for Mellers, Hertwig, and Kahneman 2001 replicated by Chandrashekar et al. 2021; root table also has a FReD/CORE conjunction-bias row.
candidate_dedupe.original_effect: FORRT narrative: d = 1.08; FReD/root evidence: d = 0.59
candidate_dedupe.replication_effect: FORRT narrative: d = 0.49; FReD/root evidence: d = 0.49
candidate_dedupe.notes: Clear duplicate/alternate support with an original-effect discrepancy that needs source-result provenance before any replacement.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: data/raw/replication_projects/fred/FReD.xlsx fred_id add_ai5 for Cialdini et al. 1975 replicated by Genschow et al. 2021.
candidate_dedupe.replication_n: 391 in FORRT narrative; FReD row contains replication source but effect is represented from test-statistic text.
candidate_dedupe.original_effect: FORRT narrative: d = 0.47; FReD stores original chi-square/test-statistic source assertions for two claims.
candidate_dedupe.notes: Likely already represented in FReD as source assertions, but not a clean already-promoted D/N row without conversion review.

candidate_dedupe.candidate_label: Sex differences in implicit maths attitudes
candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: Focused local FReD search for the exact label did not find a clean match; broad 'math' hits were unrelated or too noisy.
candidate_dedupe.notes: Potential new or alternate row, but FORRT narrative needs source-level confirmation and bibliographic grounding before promotion.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: No clean local FReD/root-table match found in the focused check.
candidate_dedupe.notes: Potential new or alternate row, but needs represented-source grounding and effect/N source text before promotion.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: Local FReD search found schizophrenia rows, but they were unrelated antipsychotic/breast-cancer database-study replications.
candidate_dedupe.notes: No clean duplicate found, but the narrative appears closer to meta-analytic/review evidence than a straightforward single source-result row.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: Local FReD search found several OSC eye-movement rows, but no clean match to the false-memory row.
candidate_dedupe.notes: Potential row, but no source-level confirmation was found locally.

candidate_dedupe.matching_fred_row_or_source: No clean local FReD/root-table match found in the focused check.
candidate_dedupe.notes: Potential row, but needs represented-source grounding and effect/N source text before promotion.

dn_assessment.row_grain: phenomenon or study-pair depending on entry
dn_assessment.notes: The conservative HTML scan found D/N-like values, but source row grain is not stable enough for automatic promotion. Several entries are already present in FReD/OSC or have alternate source assertions with different original-side N/effect choices.

recommended_next_action: Do not promote new Figure 1 rows from FORRT Reversals now. Keep the HTML scan as a manual source-result worklist only; if revisited, start with the four or five unclear candidates and acquire the original and replication source objects before adding any rows.
